text: We describe a case of robotic inducing in green fluorescence-guided total mesorectal estrogen with colon anastomosis for a locally advanced rectal cancer. The patient is a seventy-eight years old female presented with abdominal pain, cedropanic anemia, and rectal bleeding. She has a BMI of twenty-three point five, which is classified as a score of two. She had no previous abdominal surgery. The colonoscopy showed an adenocarcinoma of the lower rectum, approximately two centimeters from the dentata line, that the pretreatment MRI staged as CT, three, eight, and zero. The CT scan showed no sign of distant metastasis. After discussion in a multidisciplinary meeting, a long course neoadjuvant chemoradiation therapy was indicated. The restaging MRI performed eight weeks after the completion of neoadjuvant therapy show a moderate response stage as T two and zero. We assess the surgical difficulty by using the OMART score and we calculate a score of two over ten.

The DAVINCI XI robotic system is docked on the left side of the patient, with four robotic ports placed diagonally from the right femoral head to the left midclavicular line, crossing the left subcostal margin. A 5mm LCR assistant port is used. The procedure begins with a middle to lateral approach, targeting the left iliac vessel for a single docking procedure.

The surgeon dissects the inferior mesenteric artery approximately 1.5 centimeters from the aorta, clips it with a Hem-o-lok, and sections it using monopolar scissors. The inferior mesenteric vein is similarly dissected, clipped, and sectioned. The dissection follows a middle to lateral approach, respecting the Girota fascia, and identifies the left ureter. The dissection continues until the lower border of the pancreas is visualized to aid in splenic flexure mobilization. A sterile gauze is placed to identify the dissection plane before starting lateral to medial mobilization. The left colon is mobilized by dissecting the tall fascia using monopolar scissors and a vessel sealer.

The surgeon begins the total mesorectal excision by dissecting the posterior plane, followed by the right and left sides, and finally the anterior plane, progressing until reaching the elevator honey. A 30-degree camera is adjusted from bottom to top to enhance the view of the anorectal angle during the posterior mesorectum dissection.

The surgeon completes the mesorectal dissection towards the levator ani and performs an ileostomy. Using a long star retractor, the rectal stump is transected with monopolar cautery, five millimeters above the dentate line, preserving the sphincter. The robotic dissection plane is accessed from bottom to top, and the surgical specimen is extracted through the anus. The bulbous stump is prepared for anastomosis, ensuring lymphadenectomy. Fluorescence angiography verifies colonic stump vascularization before dividing the colon for end-to-end anastomosis. A tension-free coloanal anastomosis is constructed with twelve Vicryl-TRIO stitches.

The operative time was two hundred thirty minutes. The histological exam showed a PT three and zero carcinoma. The postoperative period was uneventful. The ileostomy was closed eight weeks after surgery.
